flashcards.front: What is the main function in C++?
flashcards.back: The main function is the entry point of any C++ program.

flashcards.front: What are header files used for in C++?
flashcards.back: Header files are used to declare functions, variables, and classes that can be used in multiple source files.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the #include directive in C++?
flashcards.back: The #include directive is used to include the contents of a header file into a source file.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between #include <filename> and #include "filename"?
flashcards.back: The first form is used for system header files, while the second form is used for user-defined header files.

flashcards.front: What is the main function signature in C++?
flashcards.back: The main function signature is int main() or int main(int argc, char* argv[]).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the return statement in the main function?
flashcards.back: The return statement is used to indicate the program's exit status.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the argc and argv parameters in the main function?
flashcards.back: The argc parameter represents the number of command-line arguments, while the argv parameter represents an array of strings containing the command-line arguments.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the #ifndef directive in C++?
flashcards.back: The #ifndef directive is used to prevent multiple inclusions of a header file.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the #define directive in C++?
flashcards.back: The #define directive is used to define a macro.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the #endif directive in C++?
flashcards.back: The #endif directive is used to end a conditional compilation block.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the using namespace std; directive in C++?
flashcards.back: The using namespace std; directive is used to bring the standard library namespace into scope.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the #include <iostream> directive in C++?
flashcards.back: The #include <iostream> directive is used to include the iostream header file, which provides input/output functions.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the std::cout object in C++?
flashcards.back: The std::cout object is used to output data to the console.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the std::cin object in C++?
flashcards.back: The std::cin object is used to input data from the console.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the std::endl object in C++?
flashcards.back: The std::endl object is used to insert a newline character and flush the output buffer.
